# Sub PEQ filters
# miniDSP-style peaking filters plus normalized biquad coefficients
# Biquad convention: y[n] = b0*x[n] + b1*x[n-1] + b2*x[n-2] - a1*y[n-1] - a2*y[n-2]

Filter 1: ON PK Fc 179.810 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 6.6667
  biquad b0=1.00030613559 b1=-1.99837744782 b2=0.9982097057 a1=-1.99837744782 a2=0.998515841294
Filter 2: ON PK Fc 106.567 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 2.6807
  biquad b0=1.00045107345 b1=-1.99776458152 b2=0.997362102798 a1=-1.99776458152 a2=0.997813176248
Filter 3: ON PK Fc 15.015 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 6.6667
  biquad b0=1.00002558115 b1=-1.9998750158 b2=0.999850400292 a1=-1.9998750158 a2=0.999875981447
Filter 4: ON PK Fc 45.776 Hz Gain +3.000 dB Q 1.7401
  biquad b0=1.00029861089 b1=-1.99854335151 b2=0.998253710483 a1=-1.99854335151 a2=0.998552321376

  biquad b0=0.999842831911 b1=-1.99912312366 b2=0.999294446138 a1=-1.99912312366 a2=0.999137278049
Filter 6: ON PK Fc 19.409 Hz Gain +2.676 dB Q 1.3569
  biquad b0=1.00014474597 b1=-1.99919618757 b2=0.99905305469 a1=-1.99919618757 a2=0.99919780066
